chief_complaint: recurrent abdominal pain and diarrhoea for 8 months
hpi_details: crampy lower abdominal pain, worse before bowel movements, relieved slightly after, 4-6 loose stools per day, occasional blood and mucus, 6kg unintentional weight loss, fatigue, no fever currently
ideas: thinks she has IBS because she's stressed at work
concerns: worried about the blood — Google told her it could be cancer
expectations: wants something to stop the diarrhoea so she can function at work
pmh: appendicectomy at 19, no other medical history
medications: none regular, occasional ibuprofen for period pain
social_hx: non-smoker, social drinker, works in finance (high stress), no recent travel

reference_soap.subjective: 34F with 8-month history of crampy lower abdominal pain, 4-6 loose stools/day with blood and mucus, 6kg weight loss, fatigue. Stress attributed by patient. No travel hx. Takes ibuprofen occasionally.
reference_soap.objective: Needs FBC (anaemia?), CRP/ESR, calprotectin, stool culture/MC&S, colonoscopy with biopsy.
reference_soap.assessment: Inflammatory bowel disease (Crohn's vs UC) most likely. DDx: infective colitis, IBS (diagnosis of exclusion), colorectal malignancy (less likely at 34 but must exclude), coeliac disease.
reference_soap.plan: Urgent outpatient or inpatient workup: bloods, stool studies, colonoscopy referral. Advise stopping NSAIDs. Address cancer anxiety with reassurance and plan. GI referral.

learning_objectives: Characterise lower GI symptoms systematically (SOCRATES for pain, stool chart features)
Identify red flag symptoms: PR blood, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms
Provide structured cancer reassurance while arranging appropriate investigation
